title: Volume of rectangular prisms with unit cubes
description: Find volume of rectangular prisms by counting unit cubes.
topic_path: Volume of rectangular prisms with unit cubes

assessment_items.question_content:
Unit cube:  
[[☃ image 1]]

**What is the volume of the rectangular prism?**  
*Some of the cubes might be hidden behind other cubes. Try to visualize all of the cubes.*

[[☃ image 2]]

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] cubic units
hints: Volume is measured by counting the number of cubic units.
So, we can count the number of unit cubes.

We can also look at the layers.

We can count $\greenD8$ cubes on each layer of the prism.


There are $\blueD3$ layers, so we can find the total number of cubes by multiplying.
  

[[☃ image 1]]

$\greenD8\times \blueD3= \Box$
The volume of the prism is $24$ cubic units.

options.alt: A cube has a visible front, right hand side, and top faces.

The front face of the rectangular prism has 6 cubes: 3 rows, 2 columns. The top face of the rectangular prism has 8 cubes: 4 rows, 2 columns. The right-hand side face of the rectangular prism has 12 cubes: 3 rows, 4 columns.

assessment_items.question_content:

So, we can count the number of unit cubes.

We can also look at the layers.

We can count $\greenD6$ cubes on each layer of the prism.


There are $\blueD3$ layers, so we can find the total number of cubes by multiplying.
  

[[☃ image 1]]

$\greenD6\times \blueD3= \Box$
The volume of the prism is $18$ cubic units.

options.alt: A cube has a visible front, right hand side, and top faces.

The front face of the rectangular prism has 9 cubes: 3 rows, 3 columns. The top face of the rectangular prism has 6 cubes: 2 rows, 3 columns. The right-hand side face of the rectangular prism has 6 cubes: 3 rows, 2 columns.
